items.title: Beijing says US Tiananmen comments 'smear' China
items.summary: BEIJING: China accused the United States on Thursday of distorting facts and smearing its political system, after Secretary of State Marco Rubio said censorship could not "erase" the memory of Beijing's 1989 Tiananmen crackdown. On June 4 that year, the Chinese government sent troops and tanks to crush protests calling for political reform in and around Beijing's Tiananmen Square. China's foreign ministry said Thursday it firmly opposed Rubio's comments.

items.title: Dancer charged with sexually abusing Malaysian teen in Beijing
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A freelance dancer claimed trial at the Sessions Court here today to charges of physical sexual abuse and committing carnal intercourse against the order of nature on a 16-year-old Malaysian boy in Beijing, China, last year. Muhammad Nazri Lanibi, 32, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read out to him before Sessions Court judge Shahrul Rizal Majid. The offence was allegedly committed about 12.30am at the China National Convention Centre Grand Hotel in Beijing, China, in June last year.

items.title: Israel: Any Hizbollah attack could bring strikes on Beirut
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israel's defence minister said Thursday that a ceasefire agreement with Lebanon grants the military the "freedom" to strike Beirut if Hizbollah attacks Israeli communities, adding that operations in southern Lebanon would continue. Katz said Israeli forces retained the "freedom of action, with American backing, to strike in Beirut in response to fire on Israeli communities and territory." Katz said the latest ceasefire agreement, brokered by Washington, "unequivocally" calls for disarming Hizbollah across Lebanon.

items.title: Dalai Lama, 90, to seek knee treatment in New Delhi
items.summary: NEW DELHI: The Dalai Lama will receive medical treatment on his left knee in New Delhi this month, his office said on Thursday, as the 90-year-old deals with a series of health issues. His followers, concerned about his health in recent years, typically hold elaborate prayers ahead of any planned medical procedures. "He will undergo medical treatment on his left knee," the Dalai Lama's office said on X, adding that he was expected to travel to India's Ladakh region by the end of June for an extended stay.

items.title: Queen visits Chow Kit school for vulnerable children and presents RM30,000 contribution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, today presented a RM30,000 cheque contribution to Sekolah Bimbingan Jalinan Kasih (SBJK) here. The donation was bestowed during Her Majesty's visit to the school as part of celebrations marking the official birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia. Receiving Her Majesty on arrival were Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, the wife of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and Education Ministry secretary-general Datuk Wan Hashim Wan Rahim.

items.title: Group wants MACC to probe transfer of elephants to Japan zoo
items.summary: Hak Asasi Hidupan Liar Malaysia (Hidup) lawyer Rajesh Nagarajan said the public deserved to know if payments were involved in the relocation of the elephants - Dara, Amoi and Kelat - to Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, and if public funds were used. At a press conference, Rajesh claimed a document issued by a company showed the cost of transporting the elephants was RM368,250. he said.

items.title: Man with rape and robbery history claims trial with wife over abuse of her three-year-old daughter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — A husband and wife pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of abusing a three-year-old girl, causing her physical injuries, last month. Nightclub bouncer Hamzi Amni Zamali, 40, and housewife Nurazwani Ahmad Rosli, 29, were jointly charged, as persons responsible for the child's care, with abusing the girl and causing physical injuries at a low-cost flat unit in Bandar Tasik Selatan here on May 23, 2026. However, should the court allow bail, she proposed bail of RM30,000 for each accused, with an additional condition requiring them to report to the nearest police station twice a month.

items.title: Zahid: Jobs for veterans by Transport, Defence ministries not tied to polls
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A joint initiative by the Transport and Defence ministries to provide jobs for armed forces veterans is unrelated to upcoming elections, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said the initiative, which offers 3,208 job opportunities in the transport and logistics sectors, aims to safeguard the welfare of military retirees. Present were Transport Minister Anthony Loke and Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin.

items.title: Delayed for a year, Penang’s revised water tariffs finally take effect on July 1
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 4 — Penang will implement new water tariff for domestic and non-domestic consumers starting July 1 after its implementation was postponed for 12 months. State Transport, Infrastructure and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said the average domestic water tariff rate for the first 35 cubic metres (m³) of water used per month has been set at RM0.93 per m³. He said the adjustment involved a maximum increase in water bills of RM2.55 per month for 82 per cent of domestic consumers or households who use 35 m³ of water or less per month, equivalent to about 8.5 sen per day.

items.title: ‘We cannot turn a blind eye’: Onn Hafiz defends RM66m health spending for Johor
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 4 — The Johor state government has spent more than RM66 million to strengthen the state's public health sector over the past four years of its administration. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said this allocation was distributed to ensure the welfare and health of approximately four million people in the state continue to be preserved. “If following the old way, the state government would not need to channel more than RM66 million to the Johor State Health Department (JKNJ).

items.title: Govt expands Rahmah sales to buffer food price hikes
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The government is expanding the Jualan Rahmah Madani initiatives, as it moves to cushion the expected rise in food prices from June onwards. Federal Agricultural Marketing Authority director-general Abdul Rashid Bahri said the programme, run jointly with the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, remains a key intervention to keep household expenses manageable while allowing market prices to adjust. Abdul Rashid said the initiative was part of a broader rollout of direct-to-consumer food programmes, including Agro Madani sales, aimed at narrowing the gap between farm gate prices and retail costs amid global and seasonal price pressures.

items.title: Facing plunging support, will Netanyahu agree to a tougher Lebanon stance?
items.summary: ISRAELI Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu faces plunging support in the electorally vital north where Hizbollah rocket fire has been heaviest, a new poll has shown, putting pressure on him to take a more hawkish stance as elections loom. Wednesday night's ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon - whether it holds or not - may not be what northern voters have in mind. The May poll by Agam Labs at Israel's Hebrew University, shared exclusively with Reuters, showed residents in the north abandoning Netanyahu's Likud more quickly than voters elsewhere and faulting him more harshly over the war in Lebanon.

items.title: Melaka GH, Masjid Tanah, Umbai and Merlimau lets patients drive-through to get their meds
items.summary: MELAKA, June 4 — Melaka has introduced drive-through pharmacy services at four healthcare facilities, enabling patients to collect their medication more conveniently without having to queue at pharmacy counters, Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh said. He said the service is available at Melaka Hospital and the Masjid Tanah, Umbai and Merlimau Health Clinics as part of efforts to improve access to healthcare services. Implemented by the Melaka Health Department under its Value Added Services (VAS) programme, the facility allows patients to obtain their medication without leaving their vehicles.

items.title: Johari says USTR's excess capacity claim may stem from third party imports
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — The US Trade Representative’s (USTR) claim of excess capacity against Malaysia may have stemmed from imports from other countries, Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said today as Putrajaya insists Malaysian manufacturers are not overproducing to flood the American market. USTR had cited suspected forced-labour concerns and alleged excess capacity among Malaysian manufacturers as reasons for a proposed 10 per cent tariff on certain Malaysian exports. “It could be that we import products that come from third parties with excess capacity, so that’s why.” Johari maintained that Malaysian manufacturers were not producing beyond market demand in an attempt to flood the US market.

items.title: King tells MACC’s new chief to tackle graft without fear or favour
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has advised newly appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman to carry out his duties with the highest level of integrity. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's Facebook page, His Majesty stressed that efforts to combat corruption must be pursued continuously, firmly and without fear or favour to safeguard integrity and uphold the rule of law. On April 25, the King consented to Abdul Halim's appointment as the new MACC chief commissioner, effective May 13.

items.title: Couple plead not guilty to abusing three-year-old girl
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A husband and wife pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of abusing a three-year-old girl, causing her physical injuries, last month. However, should the court allow bail, she proposed bail of RM30,000 for each accused, with an additional condition requiring them to report to the nearest police station twice a month. "For the court's information, the first accused (Hamzi Amni) has several previous criminal records, including rape, robbery and armed robbery.

items.title: New wild ginger species discovered in Lenggong, highlighting Perak’s biodiversity, says Saarani
items.summary: IPOH, June 4 — Researchers have discovered a new species of wild ginger, Alpinia lenggongensis, in Lenggong, a finding that further underscores Perak’s rich biodiversity and natural heritage, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said today. He said the species was discovered by researchers from Universiti Putra Malaysia and Universiti Malaysia Kelantan. “Lenggong has been a Unesco World Heritage Site since 2012 and was officially recognised as the Lenggong Unesco Global Geopark on April 27, 2026,” he said.

items.title: Govt taps military retirees to fill shortage of bus, lorry drivers and aviation technicians
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 4 — The government is turning to retired military personnel to help address labour shortages in the transport sector, including as lorry and bus drivers as well as skilled workers in the aviation maintenance industry, under a new collaboration between the Transport Ministry (MOT) and Defence Ministry (Mindef). At a joint press conference later with Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, Loke said the initiative would also help address manpower shortages across various segments of the transport industry. We do not have enough bus and truck drivers,” he said.

items.title: PKR leader defends MyKhas portal ban for MPs linked to Rafizi
items.summary: PKR deputy secretary-general Bryan Ng said the suspension was a direct consequence of Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung and Subang MP Wong Chen's "self-inflicted betrayal". "This is a fundamental matter of principles, discipline, and basic political accountability," Ng said in a statement. Lee subsequently lost access to the portal , becoming the second PKR MP affected.

items.title: 3,208 transport job opportunities opened for military veterans
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A collaboration between the Transport Ministry and the Defence Ministry is set to create 3,208 job opportunities in the transport sector. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative aims to help armed forces personnel transition into civilian careers after completing their military service. He said the collaboration between the two entities would create job opportunities for future retirees and veterans, while streamlining the conversion process of military driving licences to the relevant civilian licences.

items.title: Armizan: 8,862 vehicles registered under expanded SKDS, SKPS as of June 3
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A total of 8,862 vehicles have been registered under the enhanced implementation of the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) and Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS), which took effect on June 1. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the figure comprised 8,524 diesel-powered pick-up trucks, 329 diesel jeeps and nine petrol jeeps registered between June 1 and 3. He said the expanded eligibility under SKDS and SKPS now covers pick-up trucks and jeeps used in the land freight transport sector, subject to conditions including registration under a company or business entity and classification as commercial vehicles in the Road Transport Department's (JPJ) Motor Vehicle Licence (LKM) records.

items.title: Indonesia passes bill expanding central bank role to spur growth
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's parliament on Thursday passed sweeping legislation that doubles down on Bank Indonesia's role to support growth, while empowering lawmakers to make binding recommendations for independent financial regulators and the central bank. Those include the central bank, Financial Services Authority and Indonesia Deposit Insurance Corporation. Purbaya has pledged to uphold central bank independence.

items.title: Johor MCA rejects viral state election lineup, says candidate selection still under review
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 4 — Johor MCA today dismissed as fake a purported state election candidates’ list circulating on social media that names party president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong as the candidate for the Skudai seat. Johor MCA deputy chief Ling Tian Soon said the list is not an official party document and does not reflect any decision made by the party. He said Johor MCA has never issued any candidate list or document related to the upcoming state election.

items.title: Queen visits Chow Kit school for underprivileged children
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, visited Sekolah Bimbingan Jalinan Kasih (SBJK) for underprivileged children today as part of the king's birthday celebrations. According to a post on the Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page, the queen was received by Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and senior deputy secretary-general of the Prime Minister's Department Datuk Abd Shukor Mahmood. The Education Ministry established the school in 2013, with the aspiration of providing education for all.

items.title: Banking sector's loan quality in focus as fuel costs bite
items.summary: Kenanga Research said while gross impaired loans remain healthy at 1.4 per cent, banks are waiting for clearer signs of how higher fuel prices affect borrowers, with any weakness likely to emerge in the second quarter. "Still, even SME-concentrated banks like Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd and AMMB Holdings Bhd remain confident that delinquencies will remain manageable in the near term, supported by existing overlay buffers and the absence of meaningful stress signals thus far," it said. Kenanga Research said the cumulative loan loss coverage (LLC) for its coverage of 10 listed banks stands at 89.8 per cent, well above the industry average of 83.0 per cent.

items.title: Road bully jailed, fined RM8k and banned from driving for five years
items.summary: BALIK PULAU: A man who went viral after being caught on video making an obscene gesture against a fellow motorist was sentenced to a day's jail and fined RM8,000 by the Magistrate's Court here today. Tan Chee Gang, 37, was also barred from holding a driving licence for five years, after pleading guilty to two charges involving making obscene gestures and dangerous driving. For the second charge, Tan was sentenced to a day's jail and fined RM5,000 in default of six months' jail after he pleaded guilty to driving his car in a dangerous manner.

items.title: Malaysia draws RM250bil in digital investments in two years: Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the investment reflected strong investor confidence in Malaysia's economic prospects and its push into high-growth sectors such as artificial intelligence (AI), semiconductors and data centres. The government aims to leverage the momentum to accelerate economic restructuring and move Malaysia further up the value chain, he added. Akmal said Malaysia must move beyond traditional sector-based development and adopt a more integrated approach linking energy, digitalisation and economic growth.

items.title: Gunung Batu Putih closed off to climbers following Jaslinda's disappearance
items.summary: Perak Forestry Department director, Datuk Mohd Basri Abdul Manaf, said the closure of the climbing trail, expected to be between three and six months, is to enable the authorities to study the safety aspects and repair works along the route. This could take anywhere between three and six months," he said. Also present were Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad and Peninsular Malaysia Forestry director-general Datuk Zahari Ibrahim.

items.title: [UPDATED] Unlicensed welfare home caretaker gets 36 years for sexual abuse of minors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The caretaker of an unlicensed children's welfare home who sexually assaulted five boys has been sentenced to 36 years' jail and six strokes of the rotan by the Ampang Sessions Court. Judge Ho Kwong Chin sentenced Retna Velu, 25, after he maintained his guilty plea to nine charges involving physical and non-physical sexual abuse. Eight of the charges involved physical sexual assault, while one involved non-physical sexual assault.

items.title: Philippines AirAsia settles outstanding airport dues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) today confirms that Philippines AirAsia has paid the outstanding airport charges owed to the regulator today. "CAAP confirms that Philippines AirAsia has complied with the directive to settle its obligations this morning, June 4, subject to ongoing standard reconciliation processes," CAAP said in a statement today when contacted by Business Times. The Philippines aviation regulator said it acknowledges and appreciates Philippines AirAsia's commitment to addressing its obligations through constructive engagement and coordination with CAAP.

items.title: Wisma Putra: All registered Malaysians in Kuwait safe after drone and missile strike on airport
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Malaysia's Foreign Ministry has confirmed that there have been no reports of Malaysians being affected by the drone and missile attack targeting Kuwait International Airport in the early hours of June 3. The ministry, through the Malaysian Embassy in Kuwait, is closely monitoring the situation following the attack and has confirmed that all registered Malaysian citizens in the country are safe. “Malaysian citizens whose travel plans have been affected by the disruption are advised to contact their respective airlines directly for alternative arrangements and flight rescheduling,” the ministry said in a statement on Thursday.

items.title: TSMC boss bets big on AI growth, says he'd 'like' to hike chip prices
items.summary: HSINCHU: Taiwan's TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, is confident in its growth over the next few years, thanks to robust demand for computing power and advanced semiconductors as it rides a relentless AI boom, its chief executive officer (CEO) said on Thursday. CC Wei, speaking at TSMC's annual shareholders' meeting in the Taiwanese city of Hsinchu, said customers are still positive on the outlook for artificial intelligence, although the company continues to monitor the impact of rising component costs. Asked whether TSMC could raise prices for customers, Wei said: "I'd like to do that ... we still need to make money."

items.title: Teacher badly burned after gas cylinder explodes during leak check
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: A teacher suffered burns to 50 per cent of his face and hands after a gas cylinder exploded while he was investigating a suspected gas leak at his rented home in Kuala Ina yesterday. In the 10.26am incident, Mohamad Edham Zul, 29, a teacher at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sultan Mansor Shah in Tampin, went to the kitchen after hearing what he believed was gas escaping from a cylinder. Melaka Fire and Rescue Department public relations officer Deputy Fire Officer Mohd Hafidzatullah Rashid said Edham received emergency treatment at the scene before being taken to hospital.

items.title: Malaysia, Japan can deepen ties in clean energy and high-tech sectors, says Fadillah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Malaysia's efforts to remain economically competitive while meeting its sustainability commitments responsibly and realistically will create significant opportunities for collaboration with Japan, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. Fadillah, who is also the Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, said both countries could further strengthen cooperation, particularly in renewable energy, hydrogen development, energy efficiency, green infrastructure and sustainable industrial solutions. “Malaysia sees tremendous potential in renewable energy, green technology, digital infrastructure, advanced manufacturing, data centres, smart logistics, artificial intelligence and cross-border connectivity, sectors where Japanese expertise, innovation and global experience can complement Malaysia's strengths and ambitions.

items.title: Mexico's popular former president blasts Trump as US ties hit low
items.summary: MEXICO CITY: Former Mexican President Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador accused Washington on Wednesday of employing "interventionist and unscrupulous practices" to bolster Mexico's right-wing opposition and weaken his leftist political movement. The remarks come amid escalating tension between the United States and Mexico, now led by President Claudia Sheinbaum, whom Lopez Obrador also praised as the "best president Mexico has had in our time." One of Mexico's most popular political figures, Lopez Obrador has largely stayed out of the spotlight since leaving office in 2024, but that changed with his forceful rebuke of US President Donald Trump posted on X on Wednesday.

items.title: Make Malaysia a hostile environment for scam syndicates
items.summary: The Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation had identified at least two locations in Malaysia believed to be linked to scam syndicate operations, according to reports. The groups were suspected of relocating their bases after enforcement agencies, including the United States Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) intensified operations against scam compounds in Southeast Asia. We've also heard about Malaysians getting trapped in scam compounds in Myanmar, Cambodia and Laos.

items.title: After missing hiker case, Saarani urges climbers to engage Orang Asli communities
items.summary: IPOH, June 4 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today urged organisers to inform Tok Batin and Orang Asli communities in advance whenever hiking expeditions are conducted in forests inhabited by indigenous groups. Saarani said forest conservation and management should not be viewed solely as the responsibility of the Forestry Department, as Orang Asli communities have lived in and around these forests for generations and possess a deep understanding of the environment. Orang Asli are already serving as rangers, and we also have Orang Asli working as mountain guides,” he said.

items.title: After childbirth, back to class: Fadhlina insists pregnant students complete SPM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Teenage girls involved in out-of-wedlock pregnancies will continue to receive support to ensure they return to school and sit for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said. She said these students must be given every opportunity to continue their education and avoid dropping out of the school system. Fadhlina said that once a pregnancy is discovered, the teenager should be taken to the nearest health clinic or rural health centre to register for maternal healthcare and open a pregnancy health record or the "pink book”.

items.title: Israel far-right minister slams ceasefire with Lebanon as 'serious mistake'
items.summary: TEL AVIV: Israel's far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir criticised on Thursday a ceasefire deal with Lebanon brokered by Washington, calling it a "serious mistake." Ben Gvir said the deal, which would see Hizbollah withdraw from southern Lebanon, cannot be enforced and the Lebanese state cannot be trusted. The state of Lebanon is a partner of Hizbollah," the hawkish minister wrote.

items.title: Velesto Energy unit receives drilling contract in Thailand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Velesto Energy Bhd's subsidiary Velesto Sumber Sdn Bhd has secured a contract from Northern Gulf Petroleum Pte Ltd for offshore drilling rig services in Thailand. Under the contract, Velesto will deploy the Naga 6 rig for a drilling campaign in the Gulf of Thailand. Velesto president Megat Zariman Abdul Rahim said the award supports the continued utilisation of the company's premium jack-up rigs.

items.title: TNB, Petronas Gas sign pact for third LNG terminal project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) has inked a heads of agreement with Petronas Gas Bhd to jointly develop the third regasification terminal (RGT-3) in Lumut, Perak. This was signed through TNB's indirect wholly-owned subsidiary Integrax Bhd, a unit of TNB Power Generation Sdn Bhd. The RGT-3 capacity is being underwritten by TNB Fuel Services Sdn Bhd in stages," TNB added.

items.title: More oil escapes Hormuz, keeping traders guessing
items.summary: THE trickle of tankers exiting the Strait of Hormuz has gathered pace in recent weeks, as traders adopt stealth measures to make the crossing. The near-total closure of Hormuz stranded more than 13 million barrels of oil per day within the Gulf, forcing producers to shut down oilfields and refineries, triggering supply shortfalls and economic strain across major importing nations. An analysis of the huge volumes stored on tankers inside the Gulf suggests transit activity has quietly accelerated.

items.title: Anwar: State assembly dissolutions up to individual states [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the decision to dissolve a state assembly rests with the respective state government. Anwar was responding to questions on whether he planned to dissolve parliament to pave the way for the 16th General Election (GE16), following the dissolution of the Johor state assembly on Monday. Under election laws, the Election Commission (EC) has up to 60 days from the date of dissolution to hold the state election.

items.title: Indonesian rupiah falls to record low against US dollar
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's rupiah hit more than 18,000 per dollar for the first time Thursday as the country is stung by surging energy costs, while lawmakers passed a bill expanding oversight of the central bank that raised concerns over its independence. Bank Indonesia has in recent weeks taken several measures to try and bolster the currency by hiking interest rates, which has been hammered this year amid mounting concerns about the state of Southeast Asia's biggest economy. The parliamentary oversight will also apply to the Indonesia Deposit Insurance Corporation (LPS), tasked with guaranteeing bank deposits and ensuring banking system stability, and the Financial Services Authority (OJK) financial regulator.

items.title: Like highways, data centres bring wider economic gains beyond jobs, says Tengku Zafrul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — The misconception that data centres only generate jobs is misplaced as they bring about wider economic benefits beyond direct employment through the ecosystems they help create, said Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz. The Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) chairman said criticisms that data centres create relatively few jobs compared with manufacturing investments fail to take into account the broader economic spillovers the industry generates for the country. Tengku Zafrul said data centre operators have a responsibility not only to adopt green and sustainable energy sources, but also to help build the broader ecosystem that supports AI infrastructure in Malaysia.

items.title: PM stays focused on federal agenda amid Johor election buzz
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today stressed that the decision to dissolve a state legislative assembly rests with the respective state government. Anwar was responding to a question on whether potential state elections would disrupt the administration’s focus. The next general election is due next year.

items.title: Zahid: BN to name Johor candidates a week before nomination day
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 4 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will finalise the list of candidates for the 56 Johor State Legislative Assembly seats in the near future before announcing them a week before nomination day, said its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. In addition, he said BN would also feature a combination between existing office holders and new faces to colour the leadership talent in Johor. This time, new faces will also colour the faces of new leadership talents in Johor,” he said.

items.title: Thunderstorms and heavy rain this afternoon in parts of Selangor, Pahang, Terengganu, Johor and Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in five states until 3pm today. MetMalaysia in a statement today announced that the situation is occurring in Terengganu (Dungun and Kemaman), Pahang (Jerantut) and Selangor (Hulu Langat). Similar conditions are also expected to hit Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin) and Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Mersing, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru).

items.title: King shares vision for Kuala Lumpur’s development with Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has expressed constructive views and advice on the direction of Kuala Lumpur’s development, emphasising the importance of effective governance, efficient service delivery and continuous efforts to improve the quality of life of city residents. “His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim also received a briefing on the Kuala Lumpur Reform initiative by YB (Yang Berhormat) Hannah Yeoh, covering various aspects including security and urban planning,” the post said. Also present were Federal Territories Department director-general Datuk Muhammad Azmi Mohd Zain and Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud.

items.title: Baling police hunt four men after worker hurt in armed robbery
items.summary: BALING, June 4 — A retail shop worker in Kampung Kuala Lesung, Kuala Ketil near here was injured in a robbery involving four men, one wielding a machete,on Monday. Baling district police chief Supt Brandon Anak Richard Joe said police received a report regarding the incident involving the man, who is in his 20s, at 9.02 pm on June 1. “Investigations found that at 8.45 pm on Monday, the man was approached by four male suspects travelling in a white Perodua Bezza.

items.title: Simplified: New 10-year Malaysian passport at RM350; pay up to RM550 to RM1,350 if you lose it
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Malaysians will be able to apply for a new Malaysian passport with a 10-year validity (up from the current 5-year validity) by paying RM350, based on a new government order. In the new government order (Fees (Passports and Visas) (Amendment) Order 2026) which was gazetted and came into effect yesterday, Malaysians will have to pay RM350 when applying for the 10-year passport. Based on the Immigration Department’s website, currently there are also fines for those who are replacing their passport for the first time (RM200 fine), second time (RM500 fine), third or subsequent lost or damaged passport (RM1,000 fine).

items.title: Two quakes at 2.9-magnitude hit Ranau and Mambong within 12 hours, MetMalaysia monitoring tremors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Two weak earthquakes of magnitude 2.9 shook Ranau, Sabah and Mambong, Sarawak within 12 hours on Tuesday (June 2). Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said the earthquake in Ranau was detected at a depth of 10 kilometres (km) at 10.36 am. He said the epicentre of the earthquake was located at latitude 6.07° North and longitude 116.58° East.

items.title: Babysitter charged with murder of 23-month-old girl in Puchong, no plea recorded
items.summary: SEPANG, June 4 — A babysitter was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a 23-month-old girl under her care in Puchong last month. The accused, Nur Syuhaida Jamalluddin, 33, was alleged to have murdered Dhiya Falisha Mustaqim between 9.31 am and 11.24 am on May 18 at a unit in Apartment Kiambang, Taman Putra Perdana. The court fixed July 14 for case mention.

items.title: Anwar: Asean energy demand to surge over 60pc by 2040 as AI, urbanisation drive consumption
items.summary: He said the region is entering a period of unprecedented energy demand as governments and businesses invest heavily in digital infrastructure, advanced manufacturing and AI technologies. According to Anwar, AI can improve grid efficiency, optimise industrial energy consumption and enhance forecasting for renewable energy generation. Despite these concerns, he said AI remains a powerful tool that can support the energy transition by improving grid efficiency, forecasting renewable energy generation and optimising industrial energy consumption.

items.title: Anwar backs TNB to power Malaysia’s energy and AI ambitions
items.summary: Also present were Deputy Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad and TNB Chairman Tan Sri Abdul Razak Abdul Majid. The prime minister also gave the assurance that energy transition will be just, energy secure and AI-driven in the nation's interest. Themed “Energy & AI: The Synergy for Energy Transition”, the conference is anchored on three content pillars, namely Energy for AI, AI for Energy and Energy Transition for People, reflecting the growing interdependence between energy systems and digital technologies.

items.title: Johari: US 10pc tariff on Malaysian exports temporary, to be reviewed after 150 days
items.summary: According to the New Straits Times (NST), Johari said a new tariff structure would be determined after ongoing discussions between Malaysia and the US are concluded. According to the report, Johari said the US had proposed a 10 per cent tariff linked to forced labour concerns, while the final tariff level would depend on the outcome of negotiations on market access capacity. He stressed that the proposed 10 per cent tariff is not an additional levy on top of the temporary tariff currently being imposed.

items.title: Anwar: Crisis in Hormuz Strait shows how vulnerable fossil fuel economies are
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said growing geopolitical tensions affecting major global energy routes have exposed the vulnerabilities of relying on fossil fuels, warning that countries remain vulnerable to supply disruptions beyond their control. They are straining supply lines that power industries and keep homes running, while also triggering energy emergencies across Asean, neighbouring countries, and indeed the world. Anwar said Malaysia is also playing a leading role in advancing the Asean Power Grid, a regional initiative aimed at improving cross-border electricity connectivity and strengthening energy security across South-east Asia.

items.title: Johor police: Secret society recruitment dispute behind fatal Pekan Nanas hacking, seven to be charged with murder
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 4 — Police have linked the fatal hacking of a 22-year-old man outside an eatery in Pekan Nanas, Pontian last month to a secret society recruitment dispute, with seven suspects set to be charged with murder today. Acting Johor police chief Deputy Commissioner Hoo Chuan Huat said seven suspects have been arrested in connection with the fatal hacking and will be charged in the Magistrate’s Court today. Hoo said the seven suspects will be charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the Penal Code for acts committed with common intention.

items.title: Upko sets sights on Putatan parliamentary seat for GE16
items.summary: Upko joined the GRS coalition last month. Drawing comparisons with Sarawak, Dr Marcus said Sabah should strengthen cooperation among local parties. I hope leaders such as Hajiji (Sabah Chief Minister, Datuk Seri Panglima Hajiji Noor) and Shafie (Warisan president, Datuk Seri Panglima Mohd Shafie Apdal) will work together as united Sabah-based parties,” he said.

items.title: First group of 329 pilgrims return home as Malaysia begins 2026 Haj phase two
items.summary: SEPANG, June 4 — The first group of 329 Malaysian haj pilgrims arrived safely at the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) here at 1.13 am, marking the start of the second phase of haj operations for the 1447H/2026M Haj Season, to welcome the return of the pilgrims to the homeland. “The arrival of the first Haj pilgrims means that the second phase of this year's Haj has begun, which is the return phase of our Haj pilgrims. Alhamdulillah, everything went well and all our Haj pilgrims are in good condition.
